skill.name: d3-viz
skill.description: Creating interactive data visualisations using d3.js. This skill should be used when creating custom charts, graphs, network diagrams, geographic visualisations, or any complex SVG-based data visualisation that requires fine-grained control over visual elements, transitions, or interactions. Use this for bespoke visualisations beyond standard charting libraries, whether in React, Vue, Svelte, vanilla JavaScript, or any other environment.
skill.summary: Create interactive data visualizations with D3.js including custom charts, graphs, and network diagrams.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives from documentation files containing code examples. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code blocks with JavaScript examples for D3.js. The 'MD5 hash' and 'C2 keyword' detections are legitimate terms in documentation context (hash properties in JSON, command in D3.js API). No actual security risks present - this is a legitimate visualization skill with safe template code and reference materials.

content.user_title: Create Interactive Data Visualizations with D3.js
content.value_statement: Build custom charts, graphs, and network visualizations with precise control. This skill helps you create publication-quality interactive graphics using D3.js for any JavaScript framework.
actual_capabilities: Create custom charts and graphs with D3.js including bar charts, line charts, scatter plots, and pie charts
Build interactive visualizations with zoom, pan, brush, and hover effects
Design network diagrams, force-directed layouts, and hierarchical tree visualizations
Implement geographic visualizations with custom map projections
Generate publication-quality graphics with precise control over colors, scales, and transitions
Integrate D3.js patterns into React, Vue, Svelte, or vanilla JavaScript projects
limitations: Focused on D3.js visualization techniques - does not cover 3D graphics (use Three.js instead)
Requires basic JavaScript knowledge - does not teach fundamental programming concepts
Template code assumes client-side rendering - server-side rendering may need adaptation
Performance optimization for very large datasets (100k+ points) requires additional techniques beyond basic patterns

use_cases.title: Dashboard Builder
use_cases.description: Create interactive dashboards with real-time data visualization for business metrics, analytics, or monitoring systems

use_cases.title: Research Visualizer
use_cases.description: Transform research data into publication-quality charts and graphs for academic papers or presentations

use_cases.title: Custom Chart Designer
use_cases.description: Build unique, branded visualizations that go beyond standard charting libraries for marketing or storytelling

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bar Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a bar chart using D3.js that displays monthly sales data. Include axis labels, a title, and use the steelblue color for bars. The chart should be 800px wide and 400px tall.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a simple chart

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Line Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an interactive line chart with D3.js that shows temperature trends over time. Add hover tooltips that display values when the mouse moves over data points. Include a zoom feature for examining specific time ranges.

prompt_templates.title: Network Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a force-directed network diagram with D3.js showing relationships between entities. Nodes should be sized by importance and colored by category. Add drag functionality so users can reposition nodes.

prompt_templates.title: Stacked Area Chart with Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a stacked area chart using D3.js that compares multiple data series over time. Implement smooth transitions when data updates. Include a legend and ensure color-blind accessible palette.

output_examples.input: Create a bar chart showing sales data: Product A (150), Product B (230), Product C (180)
output_examples.output: Generates React component with D3.js bar chart including: SVG setup, scaleBand x-axis, scaleLinear y-axis, axisBottom and axisLeft, styled bars with steelblue fill, axis labels for products and values

output_examples.input: Build an interactive scatter plot with tooltips
output_examples.output: Creates D3.js scatter plot with: circle elements bound to data, hover event listeners showing tooltip div, transition effects on mouseover, calculated x/y scales with padding, axis generators and labels

output_examples.input: Make a force-directed graph of my network data
output_examples.output: Implements D3.js forceSimulation with: forceManyBody for node repulsion, forceLink for edge connections, forceCenter for positioning, draggable nodes with drag event handlers, dynamic link distance and strength calculations

best_practices: Always select and clear SVG elements before rendering to prevent memory leaks and duplicate visualizations when components re-render
Use D3.js scales for all dimensional mappings instead of manual calculations - this ensures consistent sizing and easier responsive design
Implement proper accessibility by adding ARIA labels, titles, and descriptions to SVG elements, and test color schemes for color-blind users
anti_patterns: Avoid directly manipulating React-managed DOM with D3.js after initial render - this breaks React's virtual reconciliation and causes performance issues
Do not hardcode chart dimensions - always use container queries or resize observers to make visualizations responsive to viewport changes
Never omit data validation before binding to D3 selections - unvalidated data can cause silent failures or cryptic rendering errors

faq.question: What is D3.js used for?
faq.answer: D3.js creates interactive data visualizations by binding data to DOM elements and applying transformations. Use it for custom charts, graphs, network diagrams, and geographic visualizations that require fine-grained control.

faq.question: Can I use D3.js with React?
faq.answer: Yes, D3.js works well with React. Use React for component structure and state management while using D3.js for the actual rendering and calculations. The skill includes React integration examples.

faq.question: What chart types can I create?
faq.answer: You can create bar charts, line charts, scatter plots, pie charts, histograms, box plots, treemaps, chord diagrams, force-directed networks, geographic maps, and any custom visualization you can design.

faq.question: How do I handle large datasets with D3.js?
faq.answer: For datasets over 10,000 points, use techniques like data aggregation, canvas rendering instead of SVG, quadtree indexing for interactions, and web workers for data processing. The skill references cover optimization patterns.

faq.question: What is the difference between D3.js and charting libraries?
faq.answer: D3.js provides building blocks for custom visualizations requiring manual assembly. Charting libraries like Chart.js provide pre-built charts with less control. Choose D3.js for unique, publication-quality graphics.

faq.question: How do I add animations to my visualizations?
faq.answer: Use D3.js transitions with the .transition() method to animate changes. You can interpolate attributes, styles, and transforms over time. The skill includes examples of smooth data updates and animated chart rendering.
